name: KrillApp.Trigger.CronTimer
title: Cron Timer
description: Triggers execution of child nodes on a recurring schedule defined by a cron expression. Supports 6-field format (SEC MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW) and 5-field legacy format. A background task polls every second and executes children when the schedule fires. Supports wildcards (*), ranges (1-5), steps (*/5), and lists (1,3,5).
shortDescription: Executes child nodes on a schedule defined by a cron expression.
llmPurpose: Triggers execution of child nodes on a recurring schedule defined by a cron expression.
llmBehavior: executes child nodes when schedule fires
llmInputs: cron expression string (e.g. '0 0 20 * * *' for daily at 8pm)
llmOutputs: executes all child nodes on schedule
llmTypicalUseCases: run automation at a specific time daily
llmCreationHints: Create a CronTimer when the user wants time-based recurring execution. Requires a valid cron expression. Use 6-field format: SEC MIN HOUR DOM MON DOW.

llmConnectionHints.role: root trigger node that initiates child execution on a time schedule

llmExamples: Create a cron timer that runs every day at 8pm.
Schedule this maintenance task for Sunday at midnight.
Trigger this polling sequence every 15 minutes.
